summary: Multipotent stem-like cells capable of differentiating into multiple lineages such as odontoblastic, fibroblastic, and inflammatory phenotypes characterize uncommitted mesenchymal cells present in tooth pulp tissues; this property underscores a critical role in both homeostasis and reparative responses pertinent to dentistry applications involving regeneration from endogenous resources available directly at sites requiring intervention due to trauma or disease conditions affecting hard/soft structures comprising teeth apparatuses.
